title: Sticherus farinosus
taxonomy: Sticherus farinosus belongs to the family of Osmundaceae and is classified as a type of fern. It is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all types of ferns and fern allies. The order to which it belongs is Osmundales. Within the kingdom Plantae, it is a vascular plant, meaning it has a vascular system for transporting water and nutrients. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the species.

intro: Sticherus farinosus, also known as the Stiff Horsetail, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Equisetaceae. It has been traditionally used in various folk medicines, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in treating conditions like arthritis and skin diseases. The bioactive constituents of Sticherus farinosus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
